text: Cf. Am. 4.11. The idea is not, in all probability, that of something rescued from a conflagration but that of a log which is saved from overnight to light this morning’s fire—Josue is a link between the pre-exilic and the post-exilic period.

The meaning of verses 8-10 will have depended on some kind of symbolism with which we are no longer conversant. ‘Names of good omen’; literally, ‘names of sign’. ‘The Dayspring’ seems a translation from the Septuagint Greek, which is ambiguous; probably, in accordance with the Hebrew text, it meant ‘The Branch’. See further 6.12 below, and note. It is not clear whether the ‘stone’ is a fresh metaphor for describing God’s servant, or needs some other explanation.
